name: The Kraken

distinctive_features: enormous size capable of pulling down ships
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: up to 10.5 meters (34 feet) in length

domain_specific_visual.nautical_association: often depicted near or attacking ships
domain_specific_visual.mythological_aura: surrounded by an aura of mystery and danger

functional_attributes.primary_function: legendary sea monster that terrorizes sailors
secondary_functions: symbol of the unknown and fearsome power of the sea

domain_specific_functional.mythological_role: represents the dangers of the ocean
domain_specific_functional.cultural_symbolism: embodies the fear of the unknown

relationships.description: The kraken is equated with the hafgufa in medieval lore.
The kraken legend may have originated from sightings of giant squid.

contextual_attributes.origin: Norwegian Sea, off the coast of Norway
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: A symbol of maritime folklore and the fearsome power of the sea

contrastive_constraints: NOT a small or harmless sea creature
NOT a modern or scientifically accurate depiction
